mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) predicts positive S&P 500 returns because consumer spending growth directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth fails to positively predict 20-day S&P 500 returns with a rank information coefficient below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above the zero lower bound increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity present values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) generate a stagflationary shock that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change positive and INDPRO 3-month change non-positive
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered observations, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression fails to achieve statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) predicts positive S&P 500 returns during periods of low unemployment because continued hiring signals robust labor demand and supports household income expectations, lifting consumer discretionary and broad equity indices.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth fails to positively predict 20-day S&P 500 returns when UNRATE is below 6.0%, with a rank correlation below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) above zero reflects accelerating manufacturing and utilities output, which raises industrial sector earnings forecasts and drives positive broad-market equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter because aggregate output expansion maps directly into top-line revenue growth and operating leverage for index constituents.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to positively correlate with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns, with rank information coefficient below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accommodative short-term interest rates (FEDFUNDS below 0.5%) jointly stimulate construction, durable goods demand, and credit expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered observations, or the pairwise interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (delta R-squared < 0.01) in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% annualized over six months triggers expectations of Federal Reserve tightening and higher real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change exceeds 2.5% over a 3-year rolling window.
